intro: Endlicheria punctulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae, native to Central and South America, commonly known as "Palo de Burro". The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Endlicheria punctulata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Endlicheria punctulata is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
